perturbation.original_text: However, this obligation shall not apply to any information, knowledge, data and/or know-how which: (a) Is or hereinafter becomes a part of the public knowledge through no fault of investor; or (b) investor can demonstrate was in its possession prior to the time of disclosure by TTI; or (c) investor can demonstrate was received by it from a third party who has not received the same from TTI; or (d) Is independently developed by or for investor by persons not having access to Information hereunder as shown by written records.
perturbation.changed_text: However, this obligation shall not apply to any information, knowledge, data and/or know-how which: (a) Is or hereinafter becomes a part of the public knowledge through no fault of investor; or (b) investor can demonstrate was in its possession prior to the time of disclosure by TTI; or (c) investor can demonstrate was received by it from a third party who has not received the same from TTI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(d) Is independently developed by or for investor by persons not having access to Information hereunder as shown by written records.', the investor is potentially restricted in using information independently developed without access to TTI's information. This contradicts the principle that independent creation should not be restricted by the NDA, it would have been protected originally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: However, this obligation shall not apply to any information, knowledge, data and/or know-how which: (a) Is or hereinafter becomes a part of the public knowledge through no fault of investor; or (b) investor can demonstrate was in its possession prior to the time of disclosure by TTI; or (c) investor can demonstrate was received by it from a third party who has not received the same from TTI.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the confidentiality obligation does not apply to information independently developed by the investor. The changed text removes this exception, meaning independently developed information could now be subject to the confidentiality obligation. This directly contradicts the initial allowance for independent development, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' and making the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Investor shall obligate its employees and the employees of its affiliates who shall have access to any portion of the Information to protect the confidential and proprietary nature of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Investor shall obligate its employees to protect the confidential and proprietary nature of the Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and the employees of its affiliates who shall have access to any portion of the Information', it is now unclear if the investor is obligated to ensure affiliates are bound by the NDA. The original text explicitly included affiliates, but this version only refers to employees. This undermines the comprehensive protection originally intended by the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Investor shall obligate its employees to protect the confidential and proprietary nature of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Investor must obligate its employees AND the employees of its affiliates who have access to information. The changed text removes 'and the employees of its affiliates who shall have access to any portion of the Information'. This creates a direct contradiction, since the Changed Text no longer binds affiliates to the same obligation as employees which causes the conflict/inconsistency with the contradicted text. The Contradicted Text also only obligates employees and thus the Changed Text now contradicts the Original Text. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
